# NEXO Brain

> Open-source cognitive memory system for AI agents

NEXO Brain gives AI agents persistent memory using the Atkinson-Shiffrin cognitive architecture. It provides 109+ MCP tools across 19 categories including semantic RAG, trust scoring, episodic memory, metacognitive error prevention, knowledge graph, web dashboard, and natural memory decay.

## Key Features

- Three-store memory: Sensory Register, Short-Term Memory, Long-Term Memory
- Semantic vector search with fastembed (BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5, 768 dimensions)
- Ebbinghaus forgetting curve for natural memory pruning
- Discriminative sister memory fusion
- Cognitive dissonance detection
- Trust score (0-100) for internal rigor calibration
- Metacognitive Guard: pre-edit checks with learnings and blocking rules
- Episodic memory: change logs, decisions, session diaries
- Knowledge Graph: bi-temporal entity-relationship graph with BFS traversal and D3 visualization
- Web Dashboard: 6-page visual interface at localhost:6174 (Overview, Graph, Memory, Somatic, Adaptive, Sessions)
- Cross-Platform: macOS, Linux, and Windows support with platform-appropriate process managers
- Adaptive Learned Weights: signal weights learn from real feedback via Ridge regression
- Somatic Markers: pain memory per file/area with risk scoring and validated recovery
- Adaptive Personality: 6 weighted signals (vibe, corrections, brevity, topic, tool errors, git diff)
- Evolution System: weekly self-improvement cycle with snapshot/rollback and circuit breakers
- Plugin system with hot-reload
- 100% local: SQLite + ONNX Runtime, zero cloud dependencies

## Installation

```
npx nexo-brain
```

## Documentation

- Website: https://nexo-brain.com
- Changelog: https://nexo-brain.com/changelog/
- Docs: https://nexo-brain.com/docs/
- GitHub: https://github.com/wazionapps/nexo
- Wiki: https://github.com/wazionapps/nexo/wiki
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/nexo-brain

## Architecture

Based on the Atkinson-Shiffrin model from cognitive psychology:
1. Sensory Register captures raw input (30s TTL, attention filter)
2. Short-Term Memory holds working context (rehearsal resets decay)
3. Long-Term Memory consolidates via semantic similarity (permanent)

## API

NEXO Brain exposes all functionality as MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools. Compatible with Claude Code, OpenClaw, and any MCP client.

Tool categories: Cognitive (8), Cognitive Input (5), Cognitive Advanced (8), Guard (3), Episodic (10), Sessions (4), Coordination (7), Reminders (5), Followups (4), Learnings (5), Credentials (5), Task History (3), Menu (1), Entities (5), Preferences (4), Agents (5), Backup (3), Evolution (5), Adaptive & Somatic (4), Knowledge Graph (4).
